implementation_scope.decomposition_ratio_calculation: 44 steps / ~55 unique production files = 0.8 (well under 2.5 ceiling)

external_integrations_contract_tested.note: Out of DELIVER scope; landed by DEVOPS per devops/ci-cd-pipeline.md §4.5. DELIVER uses FakePds (wiremock or recorded fixtures) for acceptance scenarios.

validation.gate: automated quality gate (6 checks) + des-verify-integrity --roadmap-only (exit 0) + nw-acceptance-designer-reviewer (29/29 scenarios covered, 0 orphans, 1:1 mapping)

notes: Step 01-01 is the ZeroR / workspace-bootstrap step per DD-2: it scaffolds every crate as todo!() so cargo build --tests succeeds and the 29 acceptance scenarios classify RED (not BROKEN).
Phase 02-03 establishes the pure-core surface that all WS scenarios depend on. The single @property test (LC-3) lives at 02-04 per DD-12.
State-delta bootstrap (DD-3) lands inside step 04-05 (DuckDB adapter), the first state-mutating adapter scenario.
Steps 05-01 through 05-17 each target a single WS scenario (one scenario = one step = one TDD cycle per nw-roadmap principle).
Phase 06 finishes with CI + supply-chain bootstrap (06-07, 06-08) so the enforcement story is in place at the end of DELIVER rather than as a separate follow-on feature.
Mutation testing (cargo-mutants ≥95% kill rate on claim-domain) is wired in 06-08 via the nightly workflow per devops/ci-cd-pipeline.md §6; it is advisory at PR time and blocking at release tag.

External integration annotation: ATProto PDS via Pact is owned by DEVOPS (tests/contract/pds/) per architecture-design.md §6.5. DELIVER uses FakePds; the real-contract suite lands as a DEVOPS deliverable outside this roadmap.

phases.name: Workspace bootstrap & RED-baseline gate
phases.description: Scaffold the entire Cargo workspace as todo!() stubs so cargo build --tests succeeds and every DISTILL scenario classifies as RED (not BROKEN). Per DD-2 the pre-DELIVER fail-for-right-reason gate runs here.

steps.name: Scaffold Cargo workspace with all 10 crates
criteria: cargo build --workspace --tests succeeds with zero compile errors
Every acceptance test compiles and classifies as RED (panics at todo!()), not BROKEN
Every crate from component-boundaries.md is a workspace member with empty stub modules
Pre-DELIVER fail-for-right-reason gate (DD-2) passes via cargo test --no-run
steps.implementation_notes: Bootstrap step (ZeroR). Every public item in every crate is a stub: pub fn|struct|trait declarations with body panic!("Not yet implemented -- RED scaffold"). Wire tests/acceptance/support/mod.rs to re-export from crates/test-support so support::* resolves. cli binary at crates/cli/src/main.rs is reachable via assert_cmd::Command::cargo_bin("openlore"). NO production logic in this step; if cargo build --tests fails, the step is incomplete. Verify by running cargo nextest run --tests 2>&1 | grep -c 'Not yet implemented' equals 29.

steps.name: Author org.openlore.* Lexicon JSON schemas
criteria: lexicons/org/openlore/claim.json and philosophy.json present matching data-models.md shape
JSON parses as valid Lexicon and includes the confidence min/max and references enum constraints
Workspace builds with the Lexicon files embedded as include_str! resources
steps.implementation_notes: Materialize the two Lexicon JSON files per data-models.md §Lexicon: org.openlore.claim and §Lexicon: org.openlore.philosophy. Field order is display-only (CID is canonical CBOR). The lexicon crate exposes them via include_str! constants so the validate function in the next phase has bytes to load. No validation logic yet — that lands in 02-01.

phases.name: Pure core — lexicon contracts
phases.description: Drive the lexicon crate's conformance scenarios (layer-2 acceptance per Mandate 9). All steps run against pure functions in-memory; zero I/O, zero CLI subprocess.

steps.name: Lexicon validates signed claim against org.openlore.claim schema
criteria: A well-formed signed claim JSON validates Ok against lexicon::claim
A claim missing a required field returns a Lexicon validation error naming the field
LC-2 passes when run via cargo nextest
steps.implementation_notes: Implement validate_claim_json against the include_str!'d Lexicon JSON. Treat the Lexicon spec as the source of truth; the serde-derived model is a convenience layer over the validated Value. Property: every required field's absence produces a typed LexiconError carrying the field name.

steps.name: Lexicon rejects out-of-range confidence at the wire boundary
criteria: Confidence 1.4 in JSON is rejected with an error naming 'confidence' and the range [0.0, 1.0]
Confidence -0.1 is rejected with the same shape
Confidence 0.0 and 1.0 (boundaries) are accepted
LC-5 passes
steps.implementation_notes: The Lexicon schema already carries minimum/maximum on confidence; the validator must honor them and produce an error message containing the field name and the numeric bounds. Defense-in-depth on top of the CLI's pre-sign check (WS-4 lands in Phase 5).

steps.name: Canonical CBOR encoding and CID computation
criteria: canonicalize(claim) emits RFC 8949 §4.2.1 canonical CBOR (sorted map keys, shortest-form ints)
compute_cid(bytes) returns a CIDv1 dag-cbor sha2-256 base32-lower string
Two invocations on equal inputs produce byte-equal CBOR and equal CIDs
steps.implementation_notes: Pure functions only; inputs in, outputs out, no I/O. Use ciborium for CBOR per technology-stack.md and the cid + multihash crates per ADR-006. This step builds the load-bearing pure pipeline that LC-3 and LC-4 verify next.

steps.name: CID is byte-stable across re-canonicalizations (proptest)
criteria: Property holds for 256 randomly generated valid claims: canonicalize twice yields byte-equal CBOR
Property holds: compute_cid produces equal CIDs across re-runs
proptest.toml pins the seed for CI determinism
LC-3 passes
steps.implementation_notes: The one @property test in slice-01 (per DD-12). Write an arb_unsigned_claim strategy producing diverse (subject, predicate, object, evidence, confidence, author, composed_at, references) values within the Lexicon constraints. Pin the proptest seed in proptest.toml so failures reproduce. PROPTEST_CASES default 256; nightly bumps to 4096 per devops/ci-cd-pipeline.md §3.7.

steps.name: CID stability against gold-fixture suite
criteria: Each tests/fixtures/gold_cids/claim_NNN.json recomputes to its frozen claim_NNN.cid
A drift in the CBOR encoder fires the test loudly
At least 5 gold fixtures cover diverse field shapes (unicode, references, empty evidence, max confidence, min confidence)
LC-4 passes
steps.implementation_notes: Catalogue fixtures that cover the canonicalization edge cases from data-models.md and exercise them as a frozen suite. Per ADR-006 Earned Trust property test 2. Fixtures are committed and signed by their CIDs; touching the encoder breaks them before a bad version ships.

steps.name: Lexicon roundtrip: compose-sign-serialize-deserialize equality
criteria: An UnsignedClaim composed from fixture_jeff_rust_memory_safety serializes and deserializes back to an equal value
The signature block survives the roundtrip unchanged
LC-1 passes
steps.implementation_notes: Implements fixture_jeff_rust_memory_safety in test-support and the serde models in lexicon. The signature block is signed by FakeIdentity::jeff; the actual signing primitive is exercised in phase 3 (claim-domain). KPI-4 invariant: zero silent normalization across the serde boundary.

steps.name: Persisted payload never contains a bucket label string
criteria: Serialized JSON of a claim at confidence 0.10, 0.50, 0.80, 0.95 contains no occurrence of 'speculative', 'weighted', 'well-evidenced', or 'triangulated'
The numeric confidence value appears as a JSON number, not a string
LC-8 passes
steps.implementation_notes: CI-failable invariant from WD-10 / D-12. The bucket-label mapping lives in claim-domain::confidence_bucket and is invoked only by render code paths; it must never touch the serde path. Verifies serializer never leaks display vocabulary into wire format.

phases.name: Pure core — claim-domain primitives
phases.description: Build the signing, verification, and reference-rules pure functions. Drive cycle and self-reference detection. These are layer-2 lexicon-conformance scenarios that depend on the pure-core APIs.

steps.name: Pure signing and verification primitives
criteria: sign(unsigned_cid, key) produces a SignatureBlock containing kid, alg=EdDSA, sig=base64url
verify(signed, pubkey) returns Ok for a valid signature and Err for a tampered one
Signing is deterministic for a given (unsigned_cid, key) pair
steps.implementation_notes: Pure functions; the key is an INPUT not state. The adapter (phase 4) loads the key from the keychain and passes it. Uses ed25519-dalek per technology-stack.md. No async, no I/O. Unit tests at the inner TDD loop establish the trust foundation for WS-6 (signature verifies) and WS-8 (PDS records verify).

steps.name: Confidence bucket helper (display-only)
criteria: confidence_bucket(0.10) returns Speculative
confidence_bucket(0.55) returns Weighted
confidence_bucket(0.86) returns WellEvidenced
confidence_bucket(0.95) returns Triangulated
Function lives in render-only module path; serde never imports it
steps.implementation_notes: Display helper per WD-10. Bucket thresholds from feature-delta WD-10. Architecture rule (enforced via cargo xtask check-arch in phase 6): the lexicon crate must NOT depend on this function. The thin separation enforces the never-persisted invariant.

steps.name: Reject self-reference in references array
criteria: reference_rules_validate(claim, None) returns Err(SelfReference) when references[].cid equals the claim's own unsigned CID
Validation runs at sign time, before signature is computed
LC-6 passes
steps.implementation_notes: ADR-008 Behavioral rule 4 + Earned Trust 2. Chicken-and-egg: compute unsigned CID once, check references against it, reject before signing. Pure function; the optional ClaimLookup parameter is None for self-reference (no storage needed).

steps.name: Reject two-hop reference cycle
criteria: When claim B (in lookup) references claim A's would-be CID, signing claim A which references B returns Err(CycleDetected)
Trait ClaimLookup is implementable by storage adapters and by in-memory test doubles
LC-7 passes
steps.implementation_notes: ADR-008 Earned Trust 3. Cross-claim cycle check requires a ClaimLookup trait declared in ports and used optionally by reference_rules_validate. The DuckDB adapter (phase 4) will implement ClaimLookup; this step ships an in-memory implementation for the test.

phases.name: Adapters and test doubles
phases.description: Implement all four effect adapters plus the test-support doubles (FakePds, FakeIdentity). Each adapter ships with a non-stub probe() per ADR-009. State-delta bootstrap (DD-3) lands on the first state-mutating adapter (DuckDB).

steps.name: Define Port traits and ProbeOutcome ADT
criteria: ports crate declares StoragePort, IdentityPort, PdsPort, ClockPort with probe() required
ProbeOutcome ADT carries Ok and Refused{reason, detail, structured} variants
ProbeRefusalReason enum is extensible and serde-serializable
Workspace compiles with adapters depending on ports
steps.implementation_notes: Per component-boundaries.md §crates/ports public surface. ProbeOutcome serializes to the structured JSON shape that tracing emits as health.startup.refused. The async trait for PdsPort uses async-trait crate; the synchronous traits do not. This is the contract surface the rest of phase 4 implements.

steps.name: System clock adapter with degenerate probe
criteria: ClockPort.now_utc() returns current RFC3339-serializable UTC instant
probe() returns ProbeOutcome::Ok unconditionally
Adapter is documented as degenerate per ADR-009
steps.implementation_notes: The simplest adapter — proves the port shape end-to-end before the harder adapters. probe() is non-stub (returns Ok with an empty Refused variant unreachable) to satisfy the structural check.

steps.name: FakeIdentity and FakeClock test doubles in test-support
criteria: FakeIdentity::jeff() yields did:plc:test-jeff with a deterministic Ed25519 key
FakeIdentity::maria() yields a second known identity for multi-author scenarios
FakeIdentity implements IdentityPort including sign/verify
author_did() strips the #fragment from the full DID
steps.implementation_notes: Per DD-6: identity treatment = fake double with known test DID. Deterministic keypair lets WS-6 and FR-2 assert signature verification against a fixed public key. tests/acceptance/support/mod.rs re-exports from test-support so existing skeleton imports resolve.

steps.name: ATProto DID identity adapter with probe
criteria: AtProtoDidAdapter loads the per-app key from OS keychain via keyring crate
sign() and verify() match the pure claim-domain primitives byte-for-byte
probe() refuses startup when DID document doesn't list the OpenLore verification method
probe() refuses startup when WSL2 fallback key file perms differ from 0600
steps.implementation_notes: Per ADR-002 probe responsibilities: DID document resolves, sentinel sign+verify, keychain accessibility, WSL2 fallback key file perms. The DID-doc update at openlore init registers the per-app key as a separate verification method per WD-12. Probe is non-stub per ADR-009 D-10 (structural enforcement).

steps.name: DuckDB storage adapter with schema migration and state-delta bootstrap
criteria: DuckDbStorageAdapter creates schema_version, claims, claim_evidence, claim_references tables idempotently on first run
write_signed_claim persists the row and the <cid>.json artifact atomically (tmp+fsync+rename)
read_signed_claim and query_by_subject return the persisted values byte-for-byte
probe() refuses startup on schema version mismatch, failed sentinel roundtrip, or fsync no-op on the storage medium
tests/common/state_delta.rs is bootstrapped per DD-3 (the first state-mutating adapter)
steps.implementation_notes: Per ADR-001 probe: schema version, sentinel roundtrip, fsync honored on the real medium (detects tmpfs/overlayfs/WSL2-DrvFs lies). Per data-models.md the JSON file and DB row are written in a single transaction-equivalent. State-delta lazy bootstrap lands here (DD-3) since DuckDB is the first observable state mutation surface. Implements ClaimLookup so phase 5 cycle checks across the local store work.

steps.name: ATProto PDS adapter and FakePds test double
criteria: AtProtoPdsAdapter implements PdsPort over atrium-api XRPC calls
create_record uses claim CID as rkey; 409/conflict treated as success returning existing at-uri
probe() refuses startup on TLS handshake failure, describeServer DID mismatch, or rkey-collision idempotency violation
FakePds in test-support records create_record calls in memory, replays get/list deterministically, and supports simulate_unreachable()
steps.implementation_notes: Per ADR-004 + architecture-design.md §6.2 probe: TLS, describeServer DID match, rkey-collision idempotency (write sentinel twice, assert no overwrite). FakePds uses wiremock per DD-6 (crafter may pick recorded-fixture replay instead). Both real and fake honor the same PdsPort contract so the CLI is unaware in test vs prod.

phases.name: CLI driver — verbs and observable contracts
phases.description: Wire the composition root and implement each CLI verb to satisfy walking-skeleton scenarios. Every step targets one or two closely-related WS scenarios. The two-prompt observable contract (ADR-003) is the load-bearing UX shape.

steps.name: openlore init bootstraps identity and DuckDB and is idempotent
criteria: First init creates identity.toml, the DuckDB file with schema, and prints 'OpenLore initialized for did:plc:test-jeff'
Re-running init prints 'already initialized for did:plc:test-jeff' and exits 0
The probe gauntlet runs at startup and refuses on any probe failure
WS-1 passes
steps.implementation_notes: Composition root (main): load config, wire adapters, run probe gauntlet, dispatch. Init verb resolves DID, derives per-app key, registers verification method, creates DuckDB schema (idempotent). TestEnv::initialized() in test-support invokes this verb so all downstream scenarios start initialized.

steps.name: Claim commands fail loudly when not initialized
criteria: Running 'openlore claim add' on a fresh environment exits non-zero
stderr names the required 'openlore init' command
WS-2 passes
steps.implementation_notes: Per ADR-009 D-9 wire-then-probe-then-use: the probe gauntlet detects the missing init state (identity.toml absent) and refuses with a structured health.startup.refused event whose user-rendered hint names 'openlore init'. This is the probe-refusal exit-2 path being exercised end-to-end.

steps.name: Compose preview contains 'not as truth' and gates on confirmation
criteria: 'openlore claim add' without stdin input prints the compose preview to stdout
Preview contains the literal text 'not as truth' (WD-6)
No file is written under ~/.local/share/openlore/claims/ before confirmation
No PDS call is made before confirmation
WS-3 passes
steps.implementation_notes: Load-bearing UX scenario. Per ADR-003 two-prompt contract: render preview, wait for Enter at sign prompt. The literal text 'not as truth' is content-frozen by AC. The local-first invariant (KPI-5) demands zero side effects before user confirmation.

steps.name: Compose rejects confidence outside [0.0, 1.0] pre-sign
criteria: '--confidence 1.4' exits non-zero with stderr naming '--confidence' and the range
No local file is written
No PDS call is made
WS-4 passes
steps.implementation_notes: Pre-sign validation in the CLI verb (NOT relying solely on the Lexicon boundary). Error message contains the flag name and the bounds. Defense-in-depth on top of LC-5.

steps.name: Bucket label is display-only; payload stores numeric
criteria: Preview for confidence 0.55 contains '0.55 (weighted)'
After signing, the on-disk JSON contains numeric 0.55 and no bucket-label string
WS-5 passes
steps.implementation_notes: Render layer invokes claim_domain::confidence_bucket for display. Persistence path (writing the signed file via the storage port) never sees the bucket label. WD-10 / D-12 invariant exercised end-to-end through the CLI subprocess.

steps.name: Sign writes atomic local file with no network call
criteria: Output contains 'Computing claim CID' and 'Written to local store'
<cid>.json appears under env.claims_dir() and its signature verifies against FakeIdentity::jeff's pubkey
No PDS call is made (assert_no_pds_call_was_made)
WS-6 passes
steps.implementation_notes: Local-first beat of the journey (KPI-5). Verb pipeline: render preview, wait for Enter, call claim_domain::canonicalize -> compute_cid -> sign, call StoragePort::write_signed_claim, render publish prompt. Atomic file write is the StoragePort adapter's responsibility (landed in 04-05).

steps.name: Re-canonicalization produces identical CIDs across runs
criteria: Two independent 'openlore claim add' runs with identical flags produce identical CIDs
Composed_at is pinned via OPENLORE_TEST_NOW env var (or equivalent) for determinism
WS-7 passes
steps.implementation_notes: Subprocess-level verification of the LC-3 property (which proved it at unit level). The clock adapter honors OPENLORE_TEST_NOW for test-only determinism without leaking into prod behavior. KPI-4 round-trip identity exercised through the CLI.

steps.name: Publish prints at-uri and retract hint
criteria: After 'Y' at the publish prompt, stdout contains 'at-uri: at://did:plc:test-jeff/org.openlore.claim/'
stdout contains the literal 'openlore claim retract' as a retract-hint (WD-6)
FakePds contains the record at the printed at-uri
WS-8 passes
steps.implementation_notes: Publish verb invoked from claim_add's Y branch (single publish code path per ADR-003). PDS rkey = CID (idempotency contract from FR-2). Retract hint in success message is content-frozen UX per WD-6.

steps.name: Publish is idempotent on re-run with same CID
criteria: Standalone 'openlore claim publish <cid>' on an already-published claim exits 0
Output indicates 'already published'
FakePds still has exactly one record for that at-uri (no duplicate)
WS-9 passes
steps.implementation_notes: Per US-003 Example 3 + ADR-003 verb publish. The PdsPort adapter treats 409/conflict as success returning the existing at-uri; the verb surfaces 'already published' rather than an error.

steps.name: PDS unreachable leaves local claim intact and retry is actionable
criteria: When pds.simulate_unreachable() is set, 'Y' at publish exits non-zero
stderr contains 'PDS' and 'retry with `openlore claim publish'
The local <cid>.json file persists intact
After pds.restore(), 'openlore claim publish <cid>' succeeds
WS-10 passes
steps.implementation_notes: Per US-003 Example 2 + KPI-5 local-first invariant. Local persistence is unconditional; network failure surfaces with an actionable retry hint naming the standalone publish verb. The retry path is what makes the local-first promise honest.

steps.name: Graph query returns just-published claim byte-for-byte
criteria: 'openlore graph query --subject <subject>' after a publish prints fields matching the fixture
Every field (subject, predicate, object, evidence, confidence, author, composed_at) matches the compose-time value byte-for-byte
WS-11 passes
steps.implementation_notes: KPI-4 the load-bearing one: zero silent normalization across compose->sign->store->query. Read path uses the same lexicon serde model as write; any normalization detected here is a bug not a feature.

steps.name: Graph query defaults local-only with footer announcement
criteria: Query output contains 'Showing local claims only'
Footer mentions '--federated' and 'slice-03'
WS-12 passes
steps.implementation_notes: Per US-004 AC #2 + WD-13. The footer signals that federation is slice-03 territory; the framing is content-frozen UX.

steps.name: Graph query empty result is explained not silent
criteria: Query for a subject with no claims prints 'No local claims about <subject>'
Output mentions '--federated' as a future affordance
Exit code is 0 (empty is not an error)
WS-13 passes
steps.implementation_notes: Per US-004 AC #3. Silent empty output is hostile; the message names the subject and points at the federation escape hatch.

steps.name: Retract publishes new counter-claim referencing original
criteria: 'openlore claim retract <cid>' publishes a NEW claim
The new claim's CID differs from the original
The new claim's references field contains {type: retracts, cid: <original_cid>}
WS-14 passes
steps.implementation_notes: Per ADR-008 §Behavioral rule 1. The retract verb constructs an UnsignedClaim with references=[{type: retracts, cid: original}], then calls into the standalone publish code path (single publish code path per ADR-003).

steps.name: Retract preserves original in both local and remote stores
criteria: After retract, the original <cid>.json file still exists locally
FakePds still returns the original record at its at-uri
graph query lists BOTH claims with 'retracted by author' annotation on the original
WS-15 passes
steps.implementation_notes: Per ADR-008 §Behavioral rules 1, 2, 3 + WD-11 no-hard-delete. Render layer joins claim_references during query and annotates retracted claims. The 'retracted by author' string is content-frozen UX.

steps.name: Corrective workflow: retract typo and publish corrected
criteria: Sequence: publish typo'd, retract typo, publish corrected, graph query
Query output lists original, retraction, AND corrected claim
Original is annotated 'retracted by author'
WS-16 passes
steps.implementation_notes: Anxiety-path scenario from gherkin-scenarios-expanded.md, resolved per DD-9 as a two-step workflow using locked verbs (claim retract + claim add with corrected evidence). No new verbs needed; this exercises the composition of existing ones.

steps.name: Calibration anxiety: cancel and re-run with lower confidence
criteria: First invocation with confidence 0.9 + EOF stdin shows '0.90 (well-evidenced)' and 'not as truth'
No file written and no PDS call after the canceled exchange
Second invocation with confidence 0.55 + Enter + 'n' signs and persists
claims_dir() contains exactly one file (the 0.55 one) and no bucket label string in it
WS-17 passes
steps.implementation_notes: Anxiety-path scenario; DD-9 binds the --edit flow to cancel + re-run. EOF on stdin at the confirm prompt exits cleanly with zero side effects (the preview already printed is fine; nothing persisted). WD-10 invariant re-asserted on the persisted file.

phases.name: Federation round-trip and CI bootstrap
phases.description: Exercise the full publish-then-query loop across multiple claims (FR-1..FR-4), then land CI workflow YAML, deny.toml, xtask checks, and the pre-commit probe-check hook so the architecture's enforceability is end-to-end.

steps.name: Federation round-trip across three predicates
criteria: Three claims with distinct subjects/predicates/objects publish successfully
Each subject queried returns its claim with the expected CID
Each CID is reachable in FakePds at the expected at-uri
FR-1 passes
steps.implementation_notes: Most thorough cross-cutting test in slice-01: exercises every adapter integrated. fixture_three_claims_different_predicates returns Rust+memory-safety, Linux+unix-philosophy, Mastodon+federation-first (or equivalent diverse triplet). Validates the slice's reason to exist.

steps.name: PDS record rkey equals claim CID
criteria: After publish, FakePds has exactly one record
record.rkey equals the parsed CID
record.collection equals 'org.openlore.claim'
FR-2 passes
steps.implementation_notes: Federation contract: rkey = CID enables the idempotency story (WS-9) AND makes at_uri reconstructible from author_did + CID (FR-3). This step locks the invariant in via an explicit subprocess assertion.

steps.name: at_uri is reconstructible from author_did and claim CID
criteria: Printed at-uri equals 'at://{author_did}/org.openlore.claim/{cid}'
DuckDB row's at_uri column equals the same value
FR-3 passes
steps.implementation_notes: Per shared-artifacts-registry rule 3. The reconstructibility is a pure function; the stored value is a cache that must equal the recomputation. Detecting drift here is a guardrail against silent at-uri corruption.

steps.name: Graph query output matches compose preview field-for-field
criteria: Every field shown in compose preview appears with the same value in query output
Timestamp byte-for-byte identical (no normalization)
Evidence URLs byte-for-byte identical
Confidence numeric byte-for-byte identical
FR-4 passes
steps.implementation_notes: KPI-4 the load-bearing one. Per data-models.md §Validation rules row 4. Helper assert_graph_query_output_matches_fixture does the structured comparison; on mismatch, prints both outputs side by side for debuggability.

steps.name: xtask check-arch enforces dependency-graph rules
criteria: 'cargo xtask check-arch' exits 0 on the current workspace
Test fixture introducing a forbidden dep (e.g. tokio in claim-domain) causes the check to fail with a clear error
Architecture invariants from component-boundaries.md are enforced
steps.implementation_notes: Per ADR-009 D-11. Parses cargo metadata, asserts claim-domain + lexicon have no transitive deps on tokio/reqwest/duckdb/keyring; no adapter-* depends on another; only cli depends on adapter-*. Runs as first CI gate per devops/ci-cd-pipeline.md §3.4.

steps.name: xtask check-probes and pre-commit hook enforce probe contract
criteria: 'cargo xtask check-probes' uses syn to walk every impl <Port> for <Adapter> block
Stub probe() bodies (Ok(_), todo!(), unimplemented!(), comment-only) are rejected
scripts/check-probes.sh runs as pre-commit hook invoking check-probes
All four adapters' probes pass the check
steps.implementation_notes: Per ADR-009 D-10 layer (b) structural check + D-11 enforcement. syn-based AST walker rejects single-expression Ok(_), todo!(), unimplemented!(), and bodies containing only TODO comments. Self-applies: there is also a test that verifies adapters actually implement non-stub probes (Earned Trust §self-application).

steps.name: Supply-chain policy via cargo-deny
criteria: deny.toml present at repo root with the license whitelist (MIT, Apache-2.0, BSD-3-Clause, Unicode-DFS-2016, ISC, Zlib)
bans on openssl, openssl-sys, serde_cbor, actix-web, axum
'cargo deny check' exits 0 on the current dependency graph
Advisories check against rustsec database is enabled
steps.implementation_notes: Per devops/ci-cd-pipeline.md §11. Bans enforce the tech-stack rejections (openssl per ADR-004 rustls choice; serde_cbor per ciborium choice). License whitelist enforces ADR-009 OSS policy.

steps.name: GitHub Actions ci.yml with commit and acceptance gates
criteria: .github/workflows/ci.yml runs on pull_request and push to main
Commit stage runs fmt, clippy -D warnings, cargo-deny, xtask check-arch, xtask check-probes, nextest run --workspace
Acceptance stage runs nextest run --test acceptance (conditional on dir presence) plus KPI-4 and KPI-5 guardrail jobs
Wall-clock target ~5 min commit, ~10 min acceptance per devops/ci-cd-pipeline.md
steps.implementation_notes: Per devops/ci-cd-pipeline.md §1-§4. Commit-stage jobs run in parallel where independent; acceptance stage gates after commit passes. Mutation testing lives in nightly.yml (advisory) per §6; release.yml is OUT OF SCOPE for slice-01 and lands when the first vX.Y.Z tag is cut.
